web.name: Balearica
web.title: Balearica | Centre Balear de Biodiversitat
web.description: Balearica is an open-access biodiversity platform for the Balearic Islands, providing curated data on taxonomy, species occurrences, genetics, and interactive maps.
home.title: Access to species data from the Balearic Islands
home.subtitle: Balearica | Centre Balear de Biodiversitat
taxon.layout.modal.title: Download center
taxon.layout.modal.checklist.help: Catalogue of species systematically organized to verify the presence and classification of organisms within the taxon.
taxon.layout.modal.occurrences.help: List of recorded occurrences for the selected taxon and all its taxonomic descendants.
taxon.layout.modal.sequences.help: List of sequences for the selected taxon and all its taxonomic descendants.
taxon.overview.description: {name}: taxonomic status overview, distribution and genetic data
taxon.overview.status.description: Explore the degree of establishment in the Balearic Islands reviewed by an expert and the IUCN conservation status for this species.
taxon.overview.statistics.description: Number of classifications at each rank from the current taxon.
taxon.overview.habitats.description: Standard terms used to describe the major habitats and systems in which the taxon occur.
taxon.overview.habitat_1.description: Continuous stand of trees and includes both forested areas (generally with a closed canopy) and wooded areas.
taxon.overview.habitat_2.description: Dominated by a grass ground cover with an overstorey of widely spaced trees and shrubs.
taxon.overview.habitat_3.description: Includes ecosystems dominated by shrubs and low woody vegetation, often interspersed with grasses or sparse trees.
taxon.overview.habitat_4.description: Comprised of grasses and broadleaved herbaceous plants, and are either without woody plants.
taxon.overview.habitat_5.description: Characterized by waterlogged soils, seasonal or permanent water, and vegetation adapted to saturated conditions.
taxon.overview.habitat_6.description: Defined by minimal soil, sparse vegetation, and extreme conditions.
taxon.overview.habitat_7.description: Limited light, stable climates, and unique ecosystems, supporting specialized species adapted to dark, nutrient-scarce conditions.
taxon.overview.habitat_8.description: Arid sandy, stony or rocky substrate landscapes with a sparse plant cover, except in depressions where water accumulates.
taxon.overview.habitat_9.description: Submergent (below extreme low tide), nearshore, on or over the continental shelf or oceanic island shelf.
taxon.overview.habitat_10.description: The pelagic habitat deeper than and beyond the continental or island shelf.
taxon.overview.habitat_11.description: The bottom habitats described in the marine deep ocean floor zone are used by both benthic and demersal organisms.
taxon.overview.habitat_12.description: Area of the shore between the extremes of high and low tides.
taxon.overview.habitat_13.description: Coastal habitats above the high tide mark.
taxon.overview.habitat_14.description: Human-made environments such as urban areas, agricultural lands, and transport corridors.
taxon.overview.habitat_15.description: Human-made water bodies such as reservoirs, canals, and ponds.
taxon.overview.habitat_16.description: Includes only non-cultivated species or those that have escaped from cultivation.
taxon.overview.habitat_17.description: A habitat type not covered by any of the other categories in the system.
taxon.overview.habitat_18.description: Not yet classified or where the habitat type is unclear.
taxon.overview.directives.description: Main directives and regulations designed to protect and conserve the taxon.
taxon.distribution.title: Distribution
taxon.distribution.subtitle: A 3D interactive map highlighting the taxon’s geographical distribution across the Balearic Islands and the surrounding relevant seas.
taxon.distribution.statistics.subtitle: Aggregated data on the taxon’s geographical distribution: seasonality, history, and sources.
taxon.distribution.statistics.graph.mont: Accumulation of occurrences month by month
taxon.genetics.sequences.description: Browse the list of genetic markers and sequences of this taxon and descendants.

about.question: What is Balearica?
about.answer_1: Balearica is a biodiversity platform, developed by the Centre Balear de Biodiversitat, is a public advanced informatics system designed to collect, manage, analyze, and share Balearic biodiversity data. The platform is built on the principle of integrating diverse data types retrieved from several sources, including field observations, museum records, genetic data, environmental data, reports, public databases, and grey literature.
about.answer_2: By integrating diverse data sources, the platform offers a comprehensive view of balearic biodiversity, enhancing scientific research and serving as a useful tool for studying biological diversity in all its dimensions. With a focus on user experience, the platform is both intuitive and accessible, catering to scientific researchers as well as the general public, and enabling thorough exploration and investigation of biodiversity. As such, we believe this platform has the potential to become an indispensable resource for researchers, conservationists, and policymakers, helping to deepen their understanding of biodiversity and bolster conservation efforts in the near future.
about.block.taxonomy.text: To develop a comprehensive and curated checklist of terrestrial and marine biodiversity for the Balearic Islands. This checklist will be compiled using data from scientific and grey literature, field observations, museum records, and expert notes. It will be aligned with the Catalogue of Life nomenclature, with taxonomic entries reviewed by experts at each biologically coherent level (e.g., family). This approach is designed to ensure high-quality taxon coverage and taxonomic consistency.
about.block.distribution.text: To produce an accurate map of biodiversity distribution within the Balearic territory. Taxa distribution data will be collected primarily from public databases, scientific and governmental reports, literature, and field investigations. The spatial information from these sources will be standardized using a common geodetic system and a list of standardized toponims. This methodology is intended to ensure high-quality geographic accuracy and consistency.
about.block.genetic.text: To create an accessible online resource for obtaining genetic information about Balearic taxa. This will be the first platform of its kind, providing easy access to standardized genetic data. Information will be sourced primarily from public databases such as NCBI and BOLD. Genetic data will be normalized by harmonizing marker codes and other metadata associated with each occurrence, ensuring consistency and ease of use.
about.block.traits.text: To provide a detailed repository of biological and ecological traits for the taxa living in the Balearic Islands. This section will compile information on various traits such as body measurements, life cycles, reproductive strategies, habitat and ecological preferences. Data will be gathered from scientific literature, field observations, and expert contributions. To ensure the accuracy and relevance of the information, biological and ecological traits will be systematically categorized and standardized according to established frameworks and terminologies.
privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.subtitle:
This Cookie Policy comes into effect on 2024-10-30.
Last updated on {update}.
privacy.info_lssi.title: LSSI Information
privacy.info_lssi.p:
This website is owned by the public entity UNIVERSITAT DE LES ILLES BALEARS (UIB), located at Cra. de Valldemossa, km 7.5, Palma (Balearic Islands), with C.I.F. Q0718001A.

This site serves to facilitate public access to the information it possesses, in accordance with the institutional purpose and the exercise of the functions conferred on the UNIVERSITAT DE LES ILLES BALEARS.
privacy.intelectual_property.title: Intellectual Property and Copyright
privacy.intelectual_property.p:
The intellectual property rights to the page, the domain name, source code, design, and navigation structure contained herein are owned by the aforementioned entity, which holds exclusive rights to exploit them in any form and, in particular, the rights of reproduction, distribution, public communication, and transformation.

The user agrees, in accordance with the Intellectual Property Law, to maintain the integrity of the work and to prevent any deformation, modification, or alteration that could harm its legitimate interests or damage its reputation. Likewise, they commit not to reproduce, copy, distribute, or publish the content of this page's information through any medium or support without prior written authorization from the owner.
privacy.policy.title: Website Terms of Use
privacy.policy.p: By accepting this privacy policy, you agree to use this website and the services offered lawfully, in accordance with these clauses, and in a way that does not harm the rights or interests of third parties or damage or cause abnormal use of the website or the services provided by it.
privacy.forms.title: Web Forms
privacy.forms.p:
In compliance with Organic Law 3/2018, of December 5, on the Protection of Personal Data and guarantee of digital rights, we inform you that the data collected will be included in one or more files managed by the UIB in the activity registry enabled for this purpose, whose purpose is to manage your request. The data requested is necessary to fulfill the aforementioned purpose, and therefore, failure to provide it prevents achieving it.

The UIB is responsible for processing the data and as such guarantees the rights of access, rectification, opposition, deletion, portability, and restriction of processing with regard to the data provided. To exercise the indicated rights, you must write to: Universitat de les Illes Balears, General Secretariat, to the attention of the Data Protection Officer, Cra. de Valldemossa, km 7.5, 07122 Palma (Illes Balears). Likewise, the UIB commits to respecting the confidentiality of your data and using it in accordance with the purpose of the files.
disclaimer.title: Disclaimer
disclaimer.subtitle:
This Disclaimer comes into effect on 2024-10-30.
Last updated on {update}.
disclaimer.usage.title: Use of the Website
disclaimer.usage.p:
Use of this website is at the user’s own risk; therefore, the UNIVERSITAT DE LES ILLES BALEARS is not responsible for any damages arising from interferences, interruptions, computer viruses, telephone failures, or disconnections due to causes beyond the public entity’s control; delays or blocks in using this electronic system caused by deficiencies or overloads in the data processing center, phone lines, Internet system, or other electrical systems; or for any other alteration that may occur in the user’s software or hardware.

Similarly, the UNIVERSITAT DE LES ILLES BALEARS will not be liable for damages caused by third parties through illegal intrusions beyond its control. It is also not responsible for damages caused by the use or misuse of the website's content or for any consequences arising from errors, defects, or omissions in the contents provided by third parties on this page.
disclaimer.contractual.title: Contractual Relationships
disclaimer.contractual.p:
Mere access to the website does not imply the existence of a contractual relationship between the user and the UNIVERSITAT DE LES ILLES BALEARS.

Consequently, the UNIVERSITAT DE LES ILLES BALEARS assumes no responsibility for any lack of veracity, incompleteness, lack of updating, and/or inaccuracy of the data or information provided on the website.
disclaimer.content.title: Website Content and Links
disclaimer.content.p:
The UNIVERSITAT DE LES ILLES BALEARS reserves the right to change, modify, add, or delete the information displayed on the website at any time, so we advise you to review this page periodically to stay updated on the changes made.

The portal assumes no responsibility for external content transmitted, hosted, or accessed from the website, as it is not required to monitor beforehand whether the linked content complies with current regulations. Nor does it guarantee the absence of viruses or other elements that may alter the user’s computer system, documents, or files, excluding any liability for damages of any kind caused to the user for this reason.

Entities that, with prior authorization from the website owner, intend to establish a link to this website must ensure that they only allow access to this page or service and do not reproduce its content and services.
disclaimer.downtime.title: Temporary Suspension of Website Accessibility
disclaimer.downtime.p: The UNIVERSITAT DE LES ILLES BALEARS reserves the right to temporarily suspend access to its website without prior notice due to the need for maintenance, repair, update, or improvement, without this implying any liability for the UNIVERSITAT DE LES ILLES BALEARS.
cookies.title: Cookie Policy
cookies.subtitle:
This Cookie Policy takes effect on 2024-10-30.
Last updated on {update}.
cookies.usage.title: Use of cookies
cookies.usage.p:
This website uses cookies to collect data for statistical purposes, and it does not identify any personal information about the user. Cookies are used to anonymously gather access information and enhance the user's accessibility to the website.

The use of session cookies (which are not stored persistently on the user's computer and disappear when the browser is closed), if used, will be limited solely to collecting technical information to identify the session and to facilitate secure and efficient navigation of the website.

Cookies used for statistical purposes collect data such as the date, browser used, operating system, and country of origin, among other things. They are managed through Google Analytics, a web analytics service provided by Google, a company based in Delaware, with its main office located at 1600 Amphitheatre Parkway, Mountain View, California, CA 94043, United States («Google»).

Google will transmit and directly store the information generated by the cookie about your use of the website (including your IP address) on servers in the United States. Google will use this information on our behalf to monitor your use of the website, compiling reports on website activity and providing services related to website activity and internet usage.

However, the user can prevent the generation of cookies using the relevant option in their browser settings. In this case, the website will remain operational, though without the benefits of personalization.
